mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.19% as of Sep 2010), the persistent gap between policy rate and equilibrium rate compresses the equity discount rate, structurally supporting forward S&P 500 returns as long as the rate stays near zero regardless of short-term fluctuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE fails to decline for three consecutive monthly releases while remaining above 9%, markets price in a stalled labor recovery, triggering short-horizon risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.0% and unchanged or rising for 3 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 5d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect disappears when tested on 2009-2012 walk-forward subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in HOUST (housing starts below 600k) and RSAFS growth deceleration signals a synchronized contraction in both durable and non-durable consumer demand that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 600 thousand units and RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample fails to show a statistically significant (p<0.10) negative mean 60d forward return relative to unconditional baseline across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock industrial recovery regime, a sustained month-over-month rise in INDPRO signals reacceleration of manufacturing output and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as markets reprice cyclical earnings growth.
failure_condition: During confirmed recovery periods, 60d forward return correlation with INDPRO 3-month change is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.03) or reverses sign in more than one of the sampled recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation falls below 1.5%, markets anticipate imminent unconventional easing (quantitative easing), producing a short-horizon positive S&P 500 reaction as risk premia compress ahead of policy action.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL 218.372 vintage Sep 2010, low inflation environment ahead of QE2
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following the trigger are not positive in a statistically significant majority (>55%) of instances across 2008-2015 sample, or sign flips after QE2 announcement precedent is excluded.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month moving average of PAYEMS month-over-month change reflects improving labor market momentum that consistently leads S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next month, independent of the absolute unemployment level.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month momentum and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below the prior 12-month trailing average signals deteriorating forward-looking housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598-599 thousand units Aug 2010, near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 50% of cases across a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero FEDFUNDS and declining CPIAUCSL inflation implies a negative or near-zero real policy rate; this combination amplifies equity valuation support beyond what either series predicts alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as investors reach for yield in real assets.
evidence_refs: fact:real policy rate near zero to negative as of Sep 2010 vintage
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) shows no incremental explanatory power over either series alone (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) in a rolling regression over any 3-year window.
